title: Long Text Integrations Make.com to Microsoft Word
excerpt:

posts.author: Jorgen (Jorgen )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All
I am using make.com to take information from Monday and put it into a Word document.
I am having problems with Long Text column.

Once it is a large number of characters in the column it continues to keep it as a long string when it goes into a word document. (One long sentence instead of paragraphs as it is laid out in Monday)

What am I doing wrong any help is appreciated.

Screenshot from Monday.

Monday Long Text1110×592 59.5 KB

Screenshot of word document.

Word Document1548×896 298 KB

posts.author: TPL (James Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Jorgen ,

Apologies for reviving a very old thread! Did you manage to find a solution for this? I’m also hoping to emulate the formatting from a long text column via make. The only workaround I can currently think of is to add a symbol at the end of each line and then use the replace function to replace that symbol with /n. Hoping you found something better though which will mean I don’t have to edit all of our current information!

Thanks.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Jorgen and @TPL !

This usually happens because of how the long text value is returned through the API. The content often comes through as a single string with escaped line breaks, so when it’s inserted into Word it loses the paragraph formatting.

In Make, try mapping the long text column’s text value and replace the line break characters before sending it to Word. A common fix is replacing \n with an actual line break.

For example in Make you can use a replace function like:

replace(long_text_field; “\n”; “\r\n”)

This converts the stored line breaks into real paragraph breaks that Word recognizes.

If the content still appears as one block, check the raw output from the monday module. Sometimes the value comes through in the value field as JSON, while the text field already contains the formatted text. Using the text field usually preserves the formatting better.

If needed, you can also add a Text parser step in Make to normalize the line breaks before passing the content into the Word template.

posts.author: TPL (James Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @drtanvisachar

Thankyou for the tip! This worked well for me, though I swapped the \r\n for {newline} in make as my next module is an html email body.
